tool.name: RNALfold
tool.description: Calculate locally stable secondary structures of RNA
tool.command: RNALfold -L $span -T $temperature -d$dangling < '$fasta_input' #if $varExists('$advancedOptions.noconversion') $advancedOptions.noconversion $advancedOptions.gquad $advancedOptions.nolp $advancedOptions.nogu $advancedOptions.noclosinggu $advancedOptions.notetra #if $advancedOptions.activateZscore #if str($advancedOptions.zscore) != "" --zscore=$advancedOptions.zscore #else --zscore #end if #end if #end if | sed -r 's/(^[.()]*) +(\(.*\)) +(.*)/\1\t\2\t\3/' > '$out_file'

param.name: span
param.label: Base pair span
param.help: Maximal distance between two paired bases (-L)

param.name: dangling
param.label: How to treat dangling end energies
param.option: unpaired bases participate in all dangling ends (2)
unpaired bases participate in one dangling end only (1)

param.name: gquad
param.label: Take G Quadruplex formation into account

param.name: noclosinggu
param.label: Allow GU pairing at the ends

param.name: notetra
param.label: Allow stabilization for loops, hairpins etc.

tool.help:
**RNALfold**
Computes locally stable RNA secondary structures with a maximal base pair span. For a sequence of length n and a base pair span of L the algorithm uses only O(n+L*L) memory and O(n*L*L) CPU time, making it practical to scan very large genomes for short RNA structures.
Output consists of secondary structure components of size <= L, one entry per line. Each line contains the predicted local structure, its energy in kcal/mol, and the starting position.
-----
**Input format**
RNALfold requires one FASTA file.
-----
**Outputs**
- Text output with dot-bracket notation and free energies of locally stable structures
